php_upload.description: File upload bypass probes targeting PHP-based servers

probes.payload: Upload .php.jpg double extension with PHP content (<?php echo 'PROBE_OK'; ?>)
probes.description: Double extension bypass — Apache may execute .php.jpg as PHP with AddHandler
Alternative PHP extension — often missing from blacklists

probes.payload: Upload with GIF89a magic bytes + PHP code as .gif
probes.description: Magic byte bypass — passes file signature validation, content is PHP
Content-Type mismatch — server-side check uses MIME, filesystem uses extension

jsp_upload.description: File upload bypass probes targeting Java-based servers

probes.payload: Upload .jsp.png double extension with JSP content
probes.description: Double extension bypass for Java application servers

probes.payload: Upload .jspx extension with JSP XML content
probes.description: JSPX alternative extension — JSP in XML format, often not blacklisted
WAR deployment — if upload dir is auto-deploy, shell becomes accessible

asp_upload.description: File upload bypass probes targeting ASP.NET/IIS servers

probes.description: Semicolon extension bypass — IIS processes as .aspx, ignores after semicolon
Content-Type mismatch bypass for classic ASP

probes.payload: Upload .config with web.config content containing handlers
probes.description: web.config upload — can reconfigure IIS to execute arbitrary extensions as ASP.NET

general.description: General file upload probes that apply across all server technologies

probes.description: SVG XSS — if served with image/svg+xml, browser executes JavaScript
HTML upload — stored XSS if served from same origin

probes.payload: Upload .htaccess to enable PHP execution in upload directory
probes.description: .htaccess upload — can reconfigure Apache to execute uploads as PHP

probes.payload: Upload oversized file (10MB+) to test size limits
probes.description: Large file upload — tests for DoS via storage exhaustion or missing size limits

polyglot_files.description: Polyglot files satisfy multiple format parsers simultaneously. PNG+PHP, JPEG+ZIP, PDF+HTML, GIF+JS — bypass MIME sniffing, file-type allowlists, and content-disposition checks. Combined with image-parser CVE chains (ImageMagick, libheif, libwebp 2024) for RCE.

probes.payload: [crafted PNG with PHP appended after IEND chunk]
probes.description: PNG+PHP polyglot — file passes image_type check, but if served via .php extension or via include(), PHP block runs. Verify by uploading then accessing as /uploads/x.php.
JPEG+phar polyglot — phar:// stream wrapper triggers unserialize. If app uses file_exists() / getimagesize() with phar:// path, RCE via gadget chain.
PDF+HTML polyglot — opens as PDF in PDF reader, as HTML in browser when served with text/html. Stored XSS via attachment-served-inline.
SVG XSS — <foreignObject><iframe srcdoc="<script>..."/></foreignObject>. Served as image/svg+xml = browser executes script in same-origin context.
ImageMagick CVE chain — MVG/MSL/PS delegates run during conversion. CVE-2016-3714 still hits unpatched libs in 2024.
libwebp / libheif heap overflow when decoding. Patched 2023-2024 but unpatched servers still vulnerable. Crashes worker process at minimum, RCE in older builds.
Zip Slip — ZIP entries with `../../etc/cron.d/x` paths. If server unpacks without sanitizing entry names, files land outside upload dir.
XLSX file with =cmd|'/c calc'!A1 cell — Excel-side RCE (CSV-injection class). Reportable when uploaded files are sent to admin emails.
